description_long: This is a large, gray military jet airplane with a streamlined fuselage and swept-back low-mounted wings. The aircraft features four engines mounted under the wings, and its tail section is characterized by a T-tail configuration with a prominent vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers mounted high. The fuselage is adorned with roundel insignias and military markings, indicating its likely use by an air force. The cockpit is positioned at the front of the fuselage, with multiple windows providing visibility for the crew. The airplane's surface appears metallic and smooth, with minimal visible surface texture, and the landing gear is not visible, suggesting it may be retractable.
description: A large gray military jet airplane with swept low-mounted wings, four underwing engines, and a T-tail, featuring air force insignias and a forward cockpit.
description_view_0: Front-left view showing the pointed nose, cockpit windows, and the full span of the swept wings with visible roundels.
description_view_1: Rear-left view highlighting the T-tail, twin engines under each wing, and military markings on the tail and wings.
description_view_2: Rear-right view emphasizing the high-mounted horizontal stabilizers and the symmetry of the engine placement.
description_view_3: Front-right view displaying the cockpit, nose, and the right-side engines and wing insignia.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing configuration with swept-back wings, no visible winglets, and a slight dihedral angle.
attribute_engine_type: Four jet engines mounted under the wings, two per side.
attribute_tail_design: T-tail configuration with a single vertical stabilizer and high-mounted horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Landing gear not visible; likely retractable tricycle configuration based on typical design.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with multiple windows, likely multi-seat.
